justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVE flagged as Critical with specific CVE reference, all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, left-pad, moment, crypto-random-string), safe_to_deploy is false, and severities are well-differentiated (Critical/High/Important/Suggestion levels).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with clear explanation of payment risk
jsonwebtoken CVE-2022-23529 specifically cited with reproduction steps
All 5 expected risks identified — exceeded the 3-of-5 threshold
crypto-random-string ESM issue correctly identified with appropriate confidence caveat
left-pad supply chain risk flagged with historical context and native replacement suggestion
moment deprecation noted at appropriate lower severity
Bonus findings: pg wide version range and missing lockfile/engine constraints add value
moment classified as Important rather than Suggestion — slightly over-weighted given it still works fine, just deprecated
safe_to_deploy false with clear pre-deployment blockers listed
